name: Melon (MelonPay) Support — Demo
description:
Demo support agent for Melon (melonpay.com) — a global business banking and cross-border payments platform. Handles three of Melon's highest-volume customer interactions: international transfer status ("where is my money?"), KYC document re-upload after rejection, and card freeze with safety confirmation. Includes phone+OTP authentication, AML escalation as a hard handoff, and explicit fallbacks for failure paths.

Flow: Entry → Intent triage → Phone+OTP auth → Router → [Transfer status lookup → delivered / in-flight / held-on-KYC / held-on-AML / failed] | [KYC: fetch pending docs → explain → request upload → submit → accepted/rejected loop] | [Card: collect last 4 → explicit confirm → freeze].

All tool calls use deterministic mock data baked into action_config — no integrations required. Pre-staged transfer references for the demo: MLN-DELIVERED, MLN-INFLIGHT, MLN-KYC, MLN-AML, MLN-FAIL.

Facts collected: intent_choice, user_phone, otp_code, transfer_reference, transfer_status, transfer_amount, transfer_rail, transfer_eta, kyc_doc_type, kyc_rejection_reason, kyc_doc_url, card_last4, verified.

default_agent_settings.system_prompt: You are Melon's customer support assistant. Melon is a global business banking and cross-border payments platform that helps businesses accept payments, hold multi-currency accounts, issue virtual and metal cards, and move money internationally via SWIFT, SEPA, ACH, and 50+ local rails. You help customers with three things: checking the status of an international transfer, completing KYC document re-uploads after rejection, and freezing or unfreezing cards. Always verify the customer's identity with phone + OTP before taking any account action. Treat anything that sounds like fraud, AML, sanctions, or suspicious activity as an immediate human handoff — never improvise on compliance. Be concise — many customers reach you over voice or WhatsApp. Use deterministic, accurate language about rails and ETAs (SEPA: same day; SWIFT: 1–4 business days; ACH: 1–2 business days). Never guess. If a tool call returns a status you don't recognize, say you'll connect a human.

scenarios.name: Melon Support — Demo

steps.name: Greeting

when.description: The customer is asking about an international transfer, payment, payout, money sent or received, or wants to know where their money is.

transitions.label: They want to check on an international transfer.

when.description: The customer is asking about KYC, identity verification, document upload, account verification, proof of address, ID upload, or a rejected document.

transitions.label: They need help with KYC documents.

when.description: The customer wants to freeze, lock, disable, suspend, or block a card. Includes 'lost my card' and 'someone has my card' if they explicitly want it frozen.

transitions.label: They want to freeze a card.

when.description: The customer reports fraud, an unauthorized transaction, suspicious activity, money laundering, or someone using their account without permission. Treat as compliance-sensitive.

transitions.label: Anything fraud / AML / sanctions goes straight to a human — agent never improvises on compliance.

when.description: The customer is ending the conversation politely with no further request.

transitions.label: Customer is wrapping up.
Still figuring out what they need.

steps.description: Open the conversation, listen for what the customer needs.
steps.say: Hi, you've reached Melon support. I can help check an international transfer, complete a KYC document re-upload, or freeze a card. What can I help you with today?

steps.name: Set Intent: Transfer

transitions.label: Routing decision saved — proceed to authentication.
Fallback: proceed to auth anyway.

steps.description: Record the routing decision so we know where to send the customer after auth.

steps.name: Set Intent: KYC

transitions.label: Routing decision saved — proceed to authentication.
Fallback: proceed to auth anyway.

steps.description: Record the routing decision so we know where to send the customer after auth.

steps.name: Set Intent: Card

transitions.label: Routing decision saved — proceed to authentication.
Fallback: proceed to auth anyway.

steps.description: Record the routing decision so we know where to send the customer after auth.

steps.name: Auth: Collect Phone

transitions.label: We have the phone number — send the OTP.
Still need the phone number.

steps.description: Ask the customer for the phone number on file before sending an OTP.
steps.say: For your security, please share the phone number on your Melon account so I can send a one-time code.

fact_requirements.name: user_phone
fact_requirements.purpose: to send a one-time passcode to the verified phone number on the Melon account

steps.name: Auth: Send OTP

transitions.label: OTP sent — collect the code.
Phone number was malformed — re-prompt.
Anything else — escalate to a human agent.

steps.description: Mock send OTP. In production this would call melon.identity.send_otp.

steps.name: Auth: Collect OTP

transitions.label: We have the OTP — verify it.
Still waiting for the code.

steps.description: Ask the customer to read back the OTP code.

fact_requirements.name: otp_code
fact_requirements.purpose: to verify the customer is the legitimate account holder before any account-specific action

steps.name: Auth: Verify OTP

transitions.label: Customer verified — route them to the right sub-flow.
Account locked — escalate to human (compliance-sensitive).
Demo simplification: invalid OTP escalates immediately. Production would loop with attempt counter.
Unexpected outcome — escalate.

steps.description: Mock OTP verification. In production this would call melon.identity.verify_otp. Demo behavior: any 4-digit numeric code passes; '0000' triggers human handoff (simulating account lock).

steps.name: Route After Auth

transitions.label: Customer originally asked about a transfer.
Customer originally asked about KYC.
Customer originally asked about card freeze.
Unknown intent — re-triage.

steps.description: Read the recorded intent_choice fact and dispatch to the right sub-flow.

steps.name: Transfer: Collect Reference

transitions.label: We have the reference — look it up.
Still need the reference.

steps.description: Ask for the Melon transfer reference (e.g., MLN-XXXXXX) or SWIFT/SEPA reference.
steps.say: Sure — what's the transfer reference? It usually starts with MLN- followed by a few characters, or you can give me the SWIFT or SEPA reference.

fact_requirements.name: transfer_reference
fact_requirements.purpose: to look up the specific transfer in Melon's payments system

steps.name: Transfer: Lookup Status

transitions.label: Transfer was delivered — close cleanly.
Transfer is in flight — explain ETA.
Transfer held on KYC — bridge into the KYC re-upload sub-flow.
Transfer held on AML — hard handoff to compliance, never improvise.
Transfer failed at the rail — explain and offer next step.
Reference didn't match — re-prompt.
Unexpected outcome — escalate.

steps.description: Mock GET /payouts/{reference}. Pre-staged demo references: MLN-DELIVERED (delivered), MLN-INFLIGHT (in-flight, SWIFT, ETA 2026-04-29), MLN-KYC (held on KYC, missing proof_of_address), MLN-AML (held on AML — escalates), MLN-FAIL (failed, invalid IBAN). Anything else returns not_found.

steps.name: Transfer: Delivered
steps.description: Confirm delivery and close.
steps.say: Glad to confirm — your transfer has settled with the recipient. Anything else I can help with?

steps.name: Transfer: In Flight
steps.description: Explain rail-specific ETA and close.
steps.say: Your transfer is moving on the network. SWIFT typically takes 1 to 4 business days; SEPA usually settles same-day; ACH is 1 to 2 business days. I've shared the expected delivery date in my last message. Anything else I can help with?

steps.name: Transfer: Failed
steps.description: Explain failure reason and that funds are refunded.
steps.say: I've shared why the transfer failed and confirmed the funds were refunded to your Melon account. You can retry from your dashboard with the corrected details. Anything else I can help with?

steps.name: KYC: Fetch Pending Docs

transitions.label: There's a doc to upload — explain it to the customer.
Nothing pending — confirm and close.
Unexpected outcome — escalate.

steps.description: Mock GET /kyc/pending. Returns the most recently rejected/pending document. Demo behavior: always returns proof_of_address as pending unless this step was reached from transfer_lookup with an existing kyc_doc_type fact already populated.

steps.name: KYC: Explain Required Doc

transitions.label: Now ask for the upload.

steps.description: Tell the customer exactly what document they need and why it was rejected.

response_policy.direct_answer_prompt: Tell the customer which KYC document is needed and why the previous one was rejected. Use the kyc_doc_type and kyc_rejection_reason facts. Then ask them to share a fresh upload link or photo.

steps.name: KYC: Request Upload

transitions.label: Customer shared a URL — submit it.
Still waiting for the upload.

steps.description: Ask the customer to share a URL or attachment for the new document.
steps.say: Could you share the document now? Either send a photo of the document directly, or paste a secure link (Dropbox, Google Drive, or our upload portal).

fact_requirements.name: kyc_doc_url
fact_requirements.purpose: to submit the customer's KYC document for re-verification

steps.name: KYC: Submit Upload

transitions.label: Document accepted — confirm and close.
Rejected — explain why and ask for a fresh upload.
Unexpected outcome — escalate.

steps.description: Mock POST /kyc/uploads. Demo behavior: URLs containing 'blurry' or 'old' are rejected; everything else is accepted with a 24-hour ETA.

steps.name: KYC: Accepted
steps.description: Confirm and close.
steps.say: Your document is in the review queue. You'll hear back within 24 hours, and any held transfers will release automatically once it's approved. Anything else?

completion.summary: KYC document submitted and queued for review.

steps.name: KYC: Nothing Pending
steps.description: Reassure the customer that nothing's needed.
steps.say: Good news — there's nothing pending on your KYC. Your account is fully verified. Anything else I can help with?

completion.summary: Confirmed no KYC documents are pending.

steps.name: KYC: Rejected, Re-Upload

transitions.label: Loop back to request a fresh upload.

steps.description: Explain rejection and ask for a fresh upload.

response_policy.direct_answer_prompt: Tell the customer the document was rejected (use the kyc_rejection_reason fact for the specific reason). Apologize briefly and ask them to send a fresh, clearly readable document.

steps.name: Card: Collect Last 4

transitions.label: We have the last 4 — confirm before freezing.
Still waiting for last 4.

steps.description: Ask the customer for the last 4 digits of the card they want to freeze.
steps.say: Of course — which card? Just give me the last 4 digits.

fact_requirements.name: card_last4
fact_requirements.purpose: to identify which card to freeze

steps.name: Card: Confirm Freeze

when.description: The customer confirms the action — yes, ok, go ahead, do it, please freeze it.

transitions.label: Customer confirmed — freeze the card.

when.description: The customer cancels or declines — no, wait, never mind, cancel, don't do it.

transitions.label: Customer cancelled — close cleanly without acting.
Still waiting for an explicit yes or no.

steps.description: Hard confirmation step before any destructive card action.

response_policy.direct_answer_prompt: Read back the card by its last 4 digits and ask the customer to explicitly confirm freezing it. Make clear this immediately blocks the card. Wait for an explicit yes or no.

steps.name: Card: Freeze

transitions.label: Card frozen — close.
Unexpected outcome — escalate.

steps.description: Mock POST /cards/{card_id}/freeze. Always succeeds in the demo.

steps.name: Card: Frozen
steps.description: Confirm and close.
steps.say: Your card is frozen. Anything else I can help with?

steps.name: Compliance Handoff (AML / Fraud)
steps.description: Hard handoff for any fraud, AML, sanctions, or compliance-sensitive case. The agent never improvises on compliance.
steps.say: I'm going to connect you to our compliance specialist now — they can help with this directly. Please hold the line.

handoff.summary: Compliance-sensitive case (AML, fraud, suspicious activity, sanctions, or held transfer). Agent escalated immediately without taking any account action.

steps.name: Human Handoff
steps.description: General-purpose escalation — anything we can't handle automatically.
steps.say: Let me get a human teammate to take this from here. One moment.

handoff.summary: Routine escalation — agent could not complete the action automatically.

steps.name: Closed
steps.description: Polite close.
steps.say: Thanks for using Melon — have a great day!

scenarios.summary: Single-scenario demo agent for Melon (MelonPay). Authenticates via phone + OTP, then routes to one of three sub-flows (transfer status, KYC re-upload, card freeze). All tool calls are deterministic mocks driven by inline action_config code; pre-staged transfer references are MLN-DELIVERED, MLN-INFLIGHT, MLN-KYC, MLN-AML, MLN-FAIL.

capabilities: Check the status of an international transfer (SWIFT, SEPA, ACH, local rails)
Walk you through KYC document re-upload after a rejection
Freeze a card after explicit confirmation
Verify your identity with phone + OTP before any account action
limitations: I can't release a transfer held under compliance review — I'll connect you to a specialist.
I can't make policy decisions on AML, fraud, or sanctions — I always escalate to a human.
I only act on the verified Melon account holder.

transfer_references.MLN-KYC: held on KYC, missing recent proof of address
transfer_references.MLN-AML: held on AML — agent hard-handoffs to compliance
transfer_references.MLN-FAIL: failed at ACH (invalid IBAN), funds refunded
